skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Guidelines and examples for drafting internal company communications, including 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Draft structured internal communications using clear company update formats.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, reconnaissance, hardcoded URLs, and command execution patterns, but review found these are false positives from prose, license text, and markdown code spans. The skill contains prompt guidelines only, with no executable code, command invocation, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts. It is safe to publish as a communication-writing helper.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography static matches are benign prose
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines are normal markdown instructions or Apache license wording. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations contain descriptive writing and license clauses, not code. There is no API call or configuration that could select a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance static matches are benign writing guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged newsletter line uses first-person company wording guidance. It does not request host information, system enumeration, environment discovery, or local machine inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is about using the word "we" in company updates. It has no semantic connection to system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL static matches are Apache license references
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The URLs point to Apache License documentation inside LICENSE.txt. They are not used by code and do not trigger any network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links appear only in standard Apache 2.0 license text. No executable file reads or calls these URLs.

low_findings.title: External command static matches are markdown file references
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backtick text names example markdown files to read. It is not Ruby or shell backtick execution and contains no command string.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are markdown formatting around paths under examples/. There is no interpreter, shell, subprocess call, or user input command construction.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often lose clarity when teams use different formats. This skill provides structured templates for concise, source-backed company communications.
actual_capabilities: Draft 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Create company-wide newsletter structures for Slack and email.
Summarize common employee questions into FAQ-style answers.
Ask for audience, purpose, tone, and formatting requirements.
Guide source gathering from Slack, email, calendar, and documents when available.
limitations: It cannot access Slack, email, calendar, or documents unless the host tool provides access.
It does not verify facts, metrics, or links without supplied source material.
It provides writing guidance only and does not approve official communications.
Company-specific tone and policy requirements must be provided by the user.

use_cases.title: Weekly Team 3P Update
use_cases.description: Convert team notes into a short executive-ready update with progress, plans, and blockers.

use_cases.title: Company Newsletter Draft
use_cases.description: Turn weekly announcements and milestones into a structured newsletter for employees.

use_cases.title: Employee FAQ Response
use_cases.description: Summarize repeated questions and draft clear answers based on official communications.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a 3P Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update for the [team name] team covering [date range]. Use these notes: [paste progress, plans, and problems]. Keep it concise for leadership.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Company Newsletter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a company-wide newsletter for [week or month]. Include sections for announcements, priority progress, leadership updates, and social updates. Use these source notes: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Build an Internal FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Create FAQ answers for employees based on these recurring questions and source details: [paste questions and facts]. Keep each answer one or two sentences.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Source-Backed Communications
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with audience, tone, and source constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an internal communication for [audience] about [topic]. Use a [formal, casual, urgent, informational] tone. Prioritize official sources, include links where provided, and flag any point that needs executive confirmation.

output_examples.input: Draft a 3P update for the platform team using notes about deployment reliability, next week's database work, and staffing blockers.
output_examples.output: A short update with an emoji title, date range, and three labeled sections: Progress, Plans, and Problems.

output_examples.input: Create a monthly newsletter from announcements about a launch, a new executive, hiring progress, and customer press.
output_examples.output: A Slack and email-ready newsletter grouped into company announcements, priority progress, leadership updates, and social updates.

output_examples.input: Answer common employee questions about an upcoming launch using official launch notes.
output_examples.output: A set of concise question-and-answer entries with uncertainty clearly marked when details need confirmation.

best_practices: Provide source notes, dates, audience, and desired tone before asking for a draft.
Mark facts that are confirmed, uncertain, or awaiting leadership approval.
Review links, metrics, and sensitive details before sending any final communication.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent company facts, metrics, or announcements.
Do not use drafts as official statements without human review.
Do not include confidential details unless the target audience is authorized.

faq.question: What types of communication can this skill draft?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Does it automatically read company tools?
faq.answer: No. It can only use Slack, email, calendar, or documents when the host environment grants access.

faq.question: Can it verify facts in a draft?
faq.answer: It can use provided sources, but users should verify facts, links, metrics, and approvals before publishing.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for executive updates?
faq.answer: Yes. The 3P format is designed for concise updates that leadership can read quickly.

faq.question: Can it match a company's tone?
faq.answer: Yes, when users provide tone guidance, examples, or style requirements.

faq.question: Does the skill run code or commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a prompt-only skill made of markdown guidance files.
